INestApplication.signature: interface INestApplication extends INestApplicationContext { listen(port: number | string, callback?: () => void): Promise<any>; use(...args: any[]): this; useGlobalPipes(...pipes: PipeTransform[]): this; useGlobalFilters(...filters: ExceptionFilter[]): this; useGlobalGuards(...guards: CanActivate[]): this; useGlobalInterceptors(...interceptors: NestInterceptor[]): this; enableCors(options?: CorsOptions): void; setGlobalPrefix(prefix: string): this; enableVersioning(options?: VersioningOptions): this; }
INestApplication.description: Interface for the NestJS HTTP application instance. Provides methods for configuring middleware, global pipes, filters, guards, interceptors, CORS, route prefixes, and versioning.

INestMicroservice.signature: interface INestMicroservice extends INestApplicationContext { listen(): Promise<any>; close(): Promise<void>; }
INestMicroservice.description: Interface for a NestJS microservice instance. Provides methods for listening and closing the microservice.

ModuleMetadata.signature: interface ModuleMetadata { imports?: Array<Type<any> | DynamicModule | Promise<DynamicModule> | ForwardReference>; controllers?: Type<any>[]; providers?: Provider[]; exports?: Array<DynamicModule | Promise<DynamicModule> | string | symbol | Provider | ForwardReference | Abstract<any> | Function>; }
ModuleMetadata.description: Metadata interface for the @Module() decorator. Defines the module's imports, controllers, providers, and exports.

DynamicModule.signature: interface DynamicModule extends ModuleMetadata { module: Type<any>; global?: boolean; }
DynamicModule.description: Interface returned by static methods like forRoot() and forRootAsync() for dynamic module registration.

Provider.signature: type Provider<T = any> = Type<T> | ClassProvider<T> | ValueProvider<T> | FactoryProvider<T> | ExistingProvider<T>
Provider.description: Union type representing all valid NestJS provider types: class, value, factory, or existing provider.

FactoryProvider.signature: interface FactoryProvider<T = any> { provide: InjectionToken; useFactory: (...args: any[]) => T | Promise<T>; inject?: Array<InjectionToken | OptionalFactoryDependency>; scope?: Scope; }
FactoryProvider.description: Provider that uses a factory function to create the provider instance. Dependencies are specified in the inject array.

ClassProvider.signature: interface ClassProvider<T = any> { provide: InjectionToken; useClass: Type<T>; scope?: Scope; }
ClassProvider.description: Provider that maps a token to a class implementation. The class will be instantiated by the IoC container.

ValueProvider.signature: interface ValueProvider<T = any> { provide: InjectionToken; useValue: T; }
ValueProvider.description: Provider that maps a token to a static value. Useful for constants, mock objects, and configuration.

ExistingProvider.signature: interface ExistingProvider<T = any> { provide: InjectionToken; useExisting: InjectionToken; }
ExistingProvider.description: Provider that creates an alias for an existing provider. The alias resolves to the same instance.

Type.signature: interface Type<T = any> extends Function { new (...args: any[]): T; }
Type.description: Represents a constructable type (class). Used throughout NestJS for type references in providers and modules.

Abstract.signature: interface Abstract<T> extends Function { prototype: T; }
Abstract.description: Represents an abstract class type. Used for abstract class-based injection tokens.

NestModule.description: Interface for modules that need to configure middleware. Implement configure() to apply middleware.

NestInterceptor.signature: interface NestInterceptor<T = any, R = any> { intercept(context: ExecutionContext, next: CallHandler<T>): Observable<R> | Promise<Observable<R>>; }
NestInterceptor.description: Interface for NestJS interceptors. Implement intercept() to add logic before/after method execution.

CanActivate.signature: interface CanActivate { canActivate(context: ExecutionContext): boolean | Promise<boolean> | Observable<boolean>; }
CanActivate.description: Interface for NestJS guards. Implement canActivate() to determine if a request should be handled.

PipeTransform.signature: interface PipeTransform<T = any, R = any> { transform(value: T, metadata: ArgumentMetadata): R; }
PipeTransform.description: Interface for NestJS pipes. Implement transform() to validate or transform input data.

ExceptionFilter.signature: interface ExceptionFilter<T = any> { catch(exception: T, host: ArgumentsHost): void; }
ExceptionFilter.description: Interface for NestJS exception filters. Implement catch() to handle specific exception types.

NestMiddleware.signature: interface NestMiddleware { use(req: any, res: any, next: (error?: any) => void): void; }
NestMiddleware.description: Interface for NestJS middleware. Implement use() with request, response, and next function parameters.

OnModuleInit.description: Lifecycle hook interface. Called once the host module's dependencies have been resolved.

OnModuleDestroy.description: Lifecycle hook interface. Called when the host module is being destroyed (app.close() invoked).

OnApplicationBootstrap.description: Lifecycle hook interface. Called once all modules have been initialized, before listening for connections.

OnApplicationShutdown.signature: interface OnApplicationShutdown { onApplicationShutdown(signal?: string): any | Promise<any>; }
OnApplicationShutdown.description: Lifecycle hook interface. Called after all connections are closed (app.close() resolves). Receives the system signal.

BeforeApplicationShutdown.signature: interface BeforeApplicationShutdown { beforeApplicationShutdown(signal?: string): any | Promise<any>; }
BeforeApplicationShutdown.description: Lifecycle hook interface. Called before the shutdown sequence. Connections are still open at this point.

ExecutionContext.signature: interface ExecutionContext extends ArgumentsHost { getClass<T = any>(): Type<T>; getHandler(): Function; }
ExecutionContext.description: Extends ArgumentsHost with helper methods to determine the current handler and class being invoked. Used in guards and interceptors.

ArgumentsHost.signature: interface ArgumentsHost { switchToHttp(): HttpArgumentsHost; switchToWs(): WsArgumentsHost; switchToRpc(): RpcArgumentsHost; getType<T extends string = ContextType>(): T; getArgs<T extends any[] = any[]>(): T; }
ArgumentsHost.description: Provides methods to retrieve handler arguments for HTTP, WebSocket, or RPC contexts. Foundation for ExecutionContext.

CallHandler.description: Represents the next interceptor or route handler in the chain. Call handle() to invoke it and get an Observable.

ArgumentMetadata.signature: interface ArgumentMetadata { type: 'body' | 'query' | 'param' | 'custom'; metatype?: Type<any>; data?: string; }
ArgumentMetadata.description: Metadata about the argument being processed by a pipe. Includes the parameter type, metatype, and data key.

HttpException.signature: class HttpException extends Error { constructor(response: string | Record<string, any>, status: number, options?: HttpExceptionOptions); getStatus(): number; getResponse(): string | object; }
HttpException.description: Base class for all HTTP exceptions. All built-in HTTP exceptions (BadRequestException, NotFoundException, etc.) extend this class.

BadRequestException.description: HTTP 400 Bad Request exception. Used when the server cannot process the request due to client error.

UnauthorizedException.description: HTTP 401 Unauthorized exception. Used when authentication is required but has failed or not been provided.

ForbiddenException.description: HTTP 403 Forbidden exception. Used when the user is authenticated but lacks permission for the resource.

NotFoundException.description: HTTP 404 Not Found exception. Used when the requested resource does not exist.

ConflictException.description: HTTP 409 Conflict exception. Used when the request conflicts with the current state of the resource.

InternalServerErrorException.description: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error exception. Used for unexpected server-side errors.

NotImplementedException.description: HTTP 501 Not Implemented exception. Used when the server does not support the requested functionality.

GoneException.description: HTTP 410 Gone exception. Used when the resource is no longer available.

PayloadTooLargeException.description: HTTP 413 Payload Too Large exception. Used when the request body exceeds the maximum allowed size.

UnprocessableEntityException.description: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity exception. Used when the request is well-formed but semantically erroneous.

ServiceUnavailableException.description: HTTP 503 Service Unavailable exception. Used when the service is temporarily unavailable.

RequestTimeoutException.description: HTTP 408 Request Timeout exception. Used when the server timed out waiting for the request.

RpcException.signature: class RpcException extends Error { constructor(error: string | object); getError(): string | object; }
RpcException.description: Base exception class for microservice RPC communication. Used instead of HttpException in microservice handlers.

WsException.signature: class WsException extends Error { constructor(error: string | object); getError(): string | object; }
WsException.description: Base exception class for WebSocket gateways. Used instead of HttpException in gateway handlers.

ControllerOptions.signature: interface ControllerOptions { path?: string | string[]; host?: string | RegExp | Array<string | RegExp>; scope?: Scope; version?: VersionValue; }
ControllerOptions.description: Options for the @Controller() decorator. Configures route path, host filtering, scope, and API version.

MiddlewareConsumer.description: Interface for configuring middleware in a module's configure() method. Chain apply().forRoutes() to specify middleware routes.

Scope.description: Enum defining provider injection scopes: DEFAULT (singleton), TRANSIENT (new per injection), REQUEST (new per request).

HttpArgumentsHost.signature: interface HttpArgumentsHost { getRequest<T = any>(): T; getResponse<T = any>(): T; getNext<T = any>(): T; }
HttpArgumentsHost.description: Provides access to HTTP-specific arguments (request, response, next) from an ArgumentsHost.

WsArgumentsHost.signature: interface WsArgumentsHost { getData<T = any>(): T; getClient<T = any>(): T; getPattern(): string; }
WsArgumentsHost.description: Provides access to WebSocket-specific arguments (data, client, pattern) from an ArgumentsHost.

RpcArgumentsHost.signature: interface RpcArgumentsHost { getData<T = any>(): T; getContext<T = any>(): T; }
RpcArgumentsHost.description: Provides access to RPC/microservice-specific arguments (data, context) from an ArgumentsHost.
